title: MVP Google-style identity login + one-time immutable role selection with backend RBAC

requirements.text: Implement an MVP-only Google-style identity login flow that results in a backend session for the caller and stores only identity fields (email, name, avatar) without assigning any role during login.

quotes: I need to implement Google OAuth login for fast testing.
Google login is only for identity (email, name, avatar).
Google login authenticates the user but does NOT assign a role.

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend provides a single "Continue with Google" login action and no password/email-verification/OTP UI.
Backend exposes a `loginWithGoogle(googleToken)` method that creates/updates a backend user record containing email, name, avatar and leaves `userType` unset (null) on first login.
After login, the app can fetch the current user via backend and determine whether role selection is still required.

requirements.text: Add a backend User model (separate from Player/Team profile models) with fields `id`, `email`, `name`, `avatar`, and `userType` where `userType` is nullable (null | player | team), and store it per authenticated caller.

quotes: User model with fields: id, email, name, avatar, userType (null | player | team)

acceptanceCriteria: Backend defines a User type that includes: id, email, name, avatar, userType (nullable).
Backend persists a mapping from the authenticated caller to the User record (MVP persistence consistent with existing in-memory state patterns in `backend/main.mo`).
`getCurrentUser()` returns the caller's User record (or null if never logged in via the Google identity flow).

requirements.text: Implement backend APIs `getCurrentUser()` and `setUserType(userType)` where `setUserType` can only be called once per user and fails if `userType` is already set.

quotes: setUserType should fail if userType already exists
Role is stored in backend and cannot be changed later.

acceptanceCriteria: `setUserType` traps/returns an error when called for a user whose `userType` is already player or team.
`setUserType` traps/returns an error when called with an invalid value (anything other than player/team).
After a successful `setUserType`, subsequent `getCurrentUser()` returns the new non-null `userType`.

requirements.text: Enforce role-based access control in the backend so that player-only APIs reject team users and team-only APIs reject player users, using the stored immutable `userType` as the source of truth (not client-provided parameters).

quotes: Player APIs must be accessible only to players, and team APIs only to teams.
All APIs must enforce role-based access (player APIs blocked for teams and vice-versa).

acceptanceCriteria: Player-only endpoints (e.g., creating posts, viewing feed, searching all hiring requirements for players, updating readiness requirements) fail for users whose `userType` is team or null.
Team-only endpoints (e.g., creating hiring requirements, talent search) fail for users whose `userType` is player or null.
Any endpoint that requires an established role fails with a clear error when `userType` is null (role not chosen).

requirements.text: Update the frontend login page to use a Google-style login button and, after login, route users based on backend `getCurrentUser()` as follows: if `userType` is null redirect to `/select-role`; if player go to Player onboarding; if team go to Team onboarding.

quotes: After login: if userType == null → show role selection screen if player → go to player onboarding if team → go to team onboarding

acceptanceCriteria: LoginPage displays a Google-style login action (text in English).
After login completes, the app calls the backend to fetch the current user and routes according to userType.
No role is assigned as part of the login action.

requirements.text: Create/adjust a dedicated role-selection route `/select-role` that is mandatory when `userType` is null and includes exactly two role choices (Player / Team) and a clear warning that the choice is permanent; selecting a role calls `setUserType` and then routes to the appropriate onboarding flow.

quotes: If role is not chosen, always redirect to a role-selection screen.
Role selection screen with two buttons: Player / Team and a warning that the choice is permanent

acceptanceCriteria: The role selection screen shows two prominent buttons: "I am a Player" and "I represent a Team" (English text).
The screen displays a warning in English: "This choice cannot be changed later" (or equivalent wording).
Choosing a role calls backend `setUserType` and on success redirects to the matching onboarding screen; on failure (already set) the UI shows an error and re-fetches current user state.

requirements.text: Add frontend routing guards so that authenticated users with `userType` unset (null) are always redirected to `/select-role`, and users with `userType` set are prevented from accessing `/select-role` (redirect to their correct onboarding/dashboard).

quotes: If role is not chosen, always redirect to a role-selection screen.

acceptanceCriteria: Any attempt to navigate to authenticated pages while `userType` is null results in a redirect to `/select-role`.
A user with `userType=player` or `userType=team` cannot remain on `/select-role` and is redirected to the correct flow.
The routing logic uses backend state (getCurrentUser / cached query) as the source of truth.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in `backend/main.mo` (single-actor architecture).
Do not modify files under `frontend/src/components/ui` or the immutable hook paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Use English for all user-facing text.
Keep the implementation MVP-only and simple (no password fields, no email verification, no phone OTP).

Platform limitation: third-party authentication providers are not supported beyond Internet Identity; if true Google OAuth token verification or server-side validation is not feasible, implement an MVP testing-compatible identity flow that still satisfies the required UX and backend role immutability.
nonGoals: Production-grade Google OAuth hardening (token verification against Google, refresh tokens, revocation, account linking/merging).
Changing user role after selection (explicitly disallowed).
Adding new backend services, databases, or external auth SDKs beyond what is feasible within the platform constraints.
